headline: S2.33 halt diagnosis is FALSIFIED. There is no BOS off-by-one bug. Lookup index 3 (' plays', the token immediately after the subject) is the CANONICAL behavior that produced the v1.5 §3 / promoted Llama results. The real S2.33 defect was the target string lacking a leading space in a DIRECT compute_z call, which bypassed the space-prepend that execute_memit applies at memit_main.py:80-82.
method: Read-source-before-authoring (gate #4) + known-baseline cross-check (gate #3 philosophy). No model dispatch this session; conclusion is closed from existing canonical artifacts on the SAME fingerprinted engine.

engine_state.note: engine UNMODIFIED and NOT to be modified — the lookup path is correct as-is

why_s233_was_wrong.claim_s233: Llama-3 auto-BOS shifts find_fact_lookup_idx from index 2 (' Jackson') to index 3 (' plays'); index 3 is a bug; fix lookup to land on ' Jackson'.
why_s233_was_wrong.refutation: The canonical, PROMOTED session 2.24 run (t1_alpha_memit_s224.ipynb) anchors compute_z at the SAME index 3 (' plays') WITH BOS present, and reproduces the v1.5 §3 endpoints exactly. Therefore index 3 is canonical, not a bug. Forcing the lookup to ' Jackson' (index 2) would diverge from every promoted Llama result and FAIL gate #3.
why_s233_was_wrong.step0_independence_argument: At optimization step 0, delta=0, so the forward pass is the unmodified model; step-0 loss/avg_prob is the model's NLL of the target at the target positions and is INDEPENDENT of lookup_idx (lookup_idx only sets where delta is injected, which is zero at step 0). Hence the 11.34-vs-18.145 step-0 gap between S2.33 and canonical CANNOT be a lookup-index effect — it is a target/sequence effect.

canonical_reference_s224.source: t1_alpha_memit_s224.ipynb (session 2.24; cell 10 builds requests; promoted into framework_finding as the 8B->3B generalization / §3 lineage)
canonical_reference_s224.target_construction: target_new str = ' ' + meta[target_new]  -> ' guitar' (leading space, 'continuation form'), single token id 17418

bo_jackson.sentence_rendered: Bo Jackson plays the instrument of<|begin_of_text|>

per_subject_lookup_indices.interpretation: index = (subject token count) + 1 in the BOS-bearing sequence, i.e. the engine canonically anchors at the first token AFTER the subject (' plays'), uniformly across subjects. This is the established behavior; cross-session comparability requires it be left unchanged.

canonical_reference_s224.gate_3_status: v1.5 §3 targets (first avg-prob ~1.6e-08, loss ~17.96->~9.35) are reproduced by s224 (1.39e-08, 18.145->~9-10) WITH index 3. Gate satisfied by the canonical setup; the S2.33 setup failed it for the reason below.

true_root_cause.name: Target string missing leading space in a DIRECT compute_z call
true_root_cause.s233_request: prompt='{} plays the instrument of', subject='Bo Jackson', target_new.str='guitar' (NO leading space)
true_root_cause.s233_comment_that_was_wrong: 'engine auto-prepends the leading space (exec line 80-82)' — TRUE for execute_memit, but S2.33 called compute_z DIRECTLY, which does not run that prepend.
true_root_cause.engine_evidence: memit/memit_main.py:80-82 -> if request['target_new']['str'][0] != ' ': requests[i]['target_new']['str'] = ' ' + request['target_new']['str']. This prepend lives in execute_memit ONLY. memit/compute_z.py has no equivalent; compute_z.py:39 tokenizes request['target_new']['str'] verbatim.
true_root_cause.mechanism: With 'guitar' (no space), compute_z.py:39 tok('guitar') tokenizes multi-token with a leading BOS; rewriting_prompts (compute_z.py:44-45) append tok.decode(target_ids[:-1]) = '<|begin_of_text|>g', corrupting the rewriting prompt to '...instrument of<|begin_of_text|>g' (visible in the S2.33 raw_stdout 'Sentence' field). Result: non-canonical step-0 loss 11.34 / avg_prob 1.26e-05.
true_root_cause.contrast: Canonical ' guitar' (1 token, id 17418) -> target_ids[:-1] decode = '<|begin_of_text|>' only -> rewriting prompt '...instrument of<|begin_of_text|>' -> step-0 loss 18.145 / avg_prob 1.39e-08.

corrected_fix.do: In the direct compute_z trajectory-capture harness, pass target_new str = ' guitar' (leading space), OR replicate execute_memit:80-82 (prepend a leading space when absent) before calling compute_z. This is a HARNESS fix, not a science-path source change (gate #5 does not apply).
corrected_fix.do_not: Do NOT modify rome/repr_tools.py or memit/compute_z.py find_fact_lookup_idx. The lookup (index 3, ' plays') is canonical. Changing it breaks cross-session comparability and fails gate #3.
corrected_fix.verification_plan_for_next_session: Load Llama-3.1-8B (pinned d04e592...), direct compute_z with corrected ' guitar' target, confirm Lookup index found: 3 AND step-0 loss ~18.1 / avg_prob ~1.4e-08, endpoints landing near v1.5 §3 (gate #3) BEFORE any data trusted or the Qwen arm run.

decisions: D-S234-1: Overturn D-S233-HALT-1. No BOS off-by-one bug exists; index 3 is canonical (s224 evidence + step-0 delta-independence).
D-S234-2: True root cause = target string lacked leading space in direct compute_z (bypassed execute_memit:80-82). Fix = pass ' guitar' (harness-level).
D-S234-3: DO NOT touch repr_tools / find_fact_lookup_idx; engine left unmodified. No gate #5 isolation needed.
D-S234-4: HALT before any new trajectory capture or the Qwen arm; surface the re-diagnosis for human re-approval (load-bearing premise change). No data produced, none promoted.
D-S234-MANIFEST-1: manifest merge STILL deferred (carry from D-S233-MANIFEST-1); no manifest writes this session.

gates.known_baseline_reproduction: Not run live this session; corrected-setup reproduction demonstrated by canonical s224 artifact. Live re-confirmation deferred to the (re-approved) next session entry, per plan above.
gates.read_source_before_authoring: PASS (cat-read of compute_z.py, repr_tools.py, memit_main.py:80-82 before any conclusion)
gates.science_path_patch_isolation: n/a (no science-path patch; fix is harness-level)
